records.title: Verify contrast and focus in context
Make every task keyboard complete
Expose accurate name, role, value, and state
Announce status without disrupting work
Do not force content into a bento grid
Do not turn every group into a card
Do not stack glass, glow, blur, border, and shadow by default
Do not use ambient gradients as default personality
Do not spend the first viewport on oversized generic hero copy
Do not animate every element into view
Do not make every control and label a pill
Prefer composition over boolean prop combinations
Build a useful baseline before enhancement
Place the server-client boundary intentionally
Give state one clear owner
Declare a browser baseline and enhance beyond it
Test real platform behavior at risk boundaries
Pair color with a redundant cue
Use color roles, not isolated swatches
Match action styling to consequence
Make empty states explain cause and next step
Treat focus behavior as a component contract
Start with the native element
Implement the complete state model
Write errors as recovery instructions
Front-load meaning in labels and headings
Use completion gates, not visual approval alone
Release visual and structural change incrementally
Choose a visual direction on explicit axes
Let content determine the composition
Decompose references before borrowing
Create distinctiveness through a repeatable system
Keep persistent labels and concise instructions
Preserve user work across failures and navigation
Validate at a useful, non-punitive moment
Use logical layout and isolate bidirectional content
Design for translation expansion and contraction
Format data with locale-aware primitives
Give each page one dominant task
Reveal complexity at the moment it becomes useful
Use icons as a consistent supporting language
Reserve image space and deliver the right bytes
Do not invent unsupported screenshot details
Do not fabricate proof, metrics, logos, or testimonials
Set line measure and density by reading task
Build hierarchy with rhythm before containers
Keep source order aligned with meaning
Use motion to explain causality and continuity
Make animations interruptible and state-safe
Provide equivalent reduced-motion behavior
Set and enforce user-centered performance budgets
Ship the least JavaScript that preserves the experience
Design with representative data and failure cases
Demand evidence before adding a feature
Begin with an outcome-first product brief
Separate requirements from preferences
Choose breakpoints where content stops working
Design for capabilities, not inferred device classes
Recompose instead of shrinking the desktop layout
Collect and expose the minimum data needed
Match destructive safeguards to consequence
Show useful errors without leaking sensitive detail
Keep metadata truthful, unique, and route-specific
Make primary content semantically discoverable
Design the adoption path with the system
Define component contracts before variants
Document decisions, not just component screenshots
Encode meaning in design tokens
Scale browser testing by feature and user risk
Test representative component states as first-class artifacts
Make typography resilient during font loading
Assign typography by semantic role
Control measure, leading, and paragraph rhythm
